General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo Jhon-Crow/audio-recorder-with-visualization --branch issue-75-e15f49b64148 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo Jhon-Crow/audio-recorder-with-visualization --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says "failing" but tools show "passing", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.

Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's HTML 404 pages saved as .png) can cause "Could not process image" errors and may crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows "HTML" or "text", the download failed and you should retry or skip the image.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/pulls/${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

   - When you need to fill forms, click buttons, or perform user interactions on web pages, use Playwright MCP tools (browser_click, browser_type, browser_fill_form).
   - When you need to test responsive design or different viewport sizes, use browser_resize from Playwright MCP.
   - When you finish using the browser, always close it with browser_close to free resources.

[2026-01-11T02:20:22.858Z] [INFO] {
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "role": "user",
    "content": [
      {
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_01Kh1U3cSMtKtdeHWaU9xBHx",
        "type": "tool_result",
        "content": "title:\tFix ofline rendering\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tJhon-Crow\nlabels:\t\ncomments:\t0\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\t\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t75\n--\n**Проблема (Текущее состояние):**\nТекущая система визуализации аудио (`AudioVisualizer`) работает исключительно в режиме реального времени, привязана к воспроизведению аудио через `HTMLAudioElement` и требует активного состояния окна/вкладки браузера (останавливается при сворачивании). Это делает невозможным:\n1.  Быстрый предпросмотр визуализации для всего файла до начала воспроизведения.\n2.  Рендеринг визуализации в фоне (например, для создания превью или при свёрнутом окне).\n3.  Достижение скорости и отзывчивости, сравнимой с профессиональными видеоредакторами (например, Sony Vegas), где визуализация строится мгновенно при загрузке или прокрутке.\n\n**Цель (Желаемое состояние):**\nРеализовать механизм **оффлайн-анализа и предварительного рендеринга** визуализации аудиофайла. Визуализация должна:\n*   Строиться **максимально быстро** после загрузки/выбора аудиофайла, до начала какого-либо воспроизведения.\n*   Быть **полностью отвязана** от процесса воспроизведения и состояния окна браузера.\n*   Позволять мгновенно отображать визуализацию для любой части трека при его прокрутке (скраббинге).\n*   Поддерживать оба режима: \"живой\" рендеринг с микрофона и \"предварительный\" для загруженных файлов.\n\n**Технические требования/предложения по реализации:**\n\n1.  **Offline Audio Context и декодирование аудиоданных:**\n    *   Использовать `OfflineAudioContext` для обработки аудио без его вывода на динамики.\n    *   Декодировать весь загруженный аудиофайл в `ArrayBuffer` с помощью `AudioContext.decodeAudioData()`.\n    *   Полученный `AudioBuffer` содержит все PCM-данные (каналы, сэмплы), доступные для синхронного анализа.\n\n2.  **Предварительный анализ и даунсэмплинг:**\n    *   Разработать функцию-анализатор, которая проходит по всему `AudioBuffer` (или его части для очень длинных файлов с шагом).\n    *   **Даунсэмплинг данных:** Не нужно анализировать каждый сэмпл (их могут быть миллионы). Для визуализации на канвасе шириной 1000px достаточно 1000-2000 точек данных.\n    *   **Алгоритм:** Разбить аудиобуфер на N сегментов (по числу пикселей по ширине графика). Для каждого сегмента рассчитать:\n        *   **RMS (Root Mean Square)** или **среднюю амплитуду** для \"основной\" формы волны.\n        *   **Пиковые значения (min/max)** для классического осциллографа.\n        *   **Спектральные данные (FFT)** для частотной визуализации. Для этого можно использовать `AnalyserNode` внутри `OfflineAudioContext`, продвигая время контекста.\n\n3.  **Кэширование результатов:**\n    *   Результаты предварительного анализа (массивы чисел для амплитуды, спектра и т.д.) должны сохраняться в памяти (например, в свойстве класса `AudioVisualizer`).\n    *   При изменении размеров канваса или параметров визуализации (масштаб, тип графика) пересчёт должен происходить из кэшированных данных, а не из исходного аудиобуфера.\n\n4.  **Быстрый рендеринг на Canvas:**\n    *   Функция отрисовки (`drawWaveform()`, `drawFrequency()`) должна работать **только с предварительно рассчитанным кэшем**, а не с `AnalyserNode` в реальном времени.\n    *   При скраббинге (перемещении ползунка) визуализация для видимой области перерисовывается моментально из кэша.\n\n5.  **Архитектурные изменения:**\n    *   **Режимы работы:** Чётко разделить логику на `mode: 'live'` (с микрофона, через `AnalyserNode` и `requestAnimationFrame`) и `mode: 'preview'` (для файлов, рендеринг из кэша).\n    *   **Интерфейс:** При загрузке файла — мгновенно запускать оффлайн-анализ и отрисовывать полную волновую форму на отдельном канвасе для навигации.\n    *   **Обратная связь:** Во время анализа большого файла показывать индикатор прогресса.\n\n**Критерии успеха (Acceptance Criteria):**\n1.  При загрузке аудиофайла формата WAV/MP3 его волновая форма (waveform) отображается на экране **в течение 1-3 секунд** (зависит от длины файла), **до нажатия кнопки Play**.\n2.  Окно приложения можно свернуть, развернуть — предварительно построенная визуализация остаётся на месте.\n3.  Перетаскивание ползунка прогресса (scrub) приводит к **мгновенной** перерисовке визуализации (осциллограф/спектр) для соответствующего момента времени.\n4.  Функциональность записи с микрофона и её \"живой\" визуализации **не сломана** и работает как прежде.\n5.  (Опционально) На больших файлах (>50 МБ) анализ запускается постепенно или с возможностью прервать.\n\n**Стек технологий для решения:**\n*   Web Audio API (`AudioContext`, `OfflineAudioContext`, `decodeAudioData`, `AudioBuffer`)\n*   Canvas API (`CanvasRenderingContext2D`)\n*   Возможна обработка в Web Worker для очень больших файлов, чтобы не блокировать основной поток.\n\n**Ссылки для вдохновения/изучения:**\n*   `OfflineAudioContext`: [MDN Documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/OfflineAudioContext)\n*   `decodeAudioData`: [MDN Documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/BaseAudioContext/decodeAudioData)\n*   Принцип работы waveform в Audacity или аудиоредакторах.",

Визуализация должна:\n*   Строиться **максимально быстро** после загрузки/выбора аудиофайла, до начала какого-либо воспроизведения.\n*   Быть **полностью отвязана** от процесса воспроизведения и состояния окна браузера.\n*   Позволять мгновенно отображать визуализацию для любой части трека при его прокрутке (скраббинге).\n*   Поддерживать оба режима: \"живой\" рендеринг с микрофона и \"предварительный\" для загруженных файлов.\n\n**Технические требования/предложения по реализации:**\n\n1.  **Offline Audio Context и декодирование аудиоданных:**\n    *   Использовать `OfflineAudioContext` для обработки аудио без его вывода на динамики.\n    *   Декодировать весь загруженный аудиофайл в `ArrayBuffer` с помощью `AudioContext.decodeAudioData()`.\n    *   Полученный `AudioBuffer` содержит все PCM-данные (каналы, сэмплы), доступные для синхронного анализа.\n\n2.  **Предварительный анализ и даунсэмплинг:**\n    *   Разработать функцию-анализатор, которая проходит по всему `AudioBuffer` (или его части для очень длинных файлов с шагом).\n    *   **Даунсэмплинг данных:** Не нужно анализировать каждый сэмпл (их могут быть миллионы). format,\n      fps,\n      videoBitrate,\n      audioBitrate,\n    });\n\n    // Start playback\n    audioElement.play();\n    this.log('Started playback and recording');\n\n    // Render loop with improved timing and reliability\n    const frameInterval = 1000 / fps;\n    let lastFrameTime = 0;\n    let frameCount = 0;\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      let hasErrored = false;\n\n      const cleanup = (): void => {\n        visualizer.destroy();\n        analyzer.destroy();\n        if (audioSource instanceof File) {\n          URL.revokeObjectURL(audioElement.src);\n        }\n      };\n\n      const renderFrame = (): void => {\n        if (hasErrored) return;\n\n        // Check for cancellation\n        if (this.isCancelled) {\n          hasErrored = true;\n          videoRecorder.cancel();\n          cleanup();\n          reject(new Error('Conversion cancelled by user'));\n          return;\n        }\n\n        try {\n          const now = performance.now();\n\n          if (now - lastFrameTime >= frameInterval) {\n            lastFrameTime = now;\n            frameCount++;\n\n            const data: VisualizationData = {\n              timeDomainData: analyzer.getTimeDomainData(),\n              frequencyData: analyzer.getFrequencyData(),\n              timestamp: now,\n              width: canvas.width,\n              height: canvas.height,\n              sampleRate: analyzer.sampleRate,\n              fftSize: analyzer.fftSize,\n            };\n\n            visualizer.draw(ctx, data);\n\n            // Report progress\n            if (onProgress && duration > 0) {\n              const progress = Math.min(audioElement.currentTime / duration, 1);\n              onProgress(progress);\n            }\n          }\n\n          // Continue until audio ends or cancelled\n          if (!audioElement.ended && !audioElement.paused && !this.isCancelled) {\n            requestAnimationFrame(renderFrame);\n          } else {\n            // Audio ended, stop recording\n            this.log('Audio playback ended after', frameCount, 'frames');\n\n            // Wait longer to ensure all frames are captured by MediaRecorder\n            setTimeout(async () => {\n              if (hasErrored) return;\n\n              try {\n                const blob = await videoRecorder.stop();\n\n                // Cleanup\n                cleanup();\n\n                if (onProgress) {\n                  onProgress(1);\n                }\n\n                this.log('Conversion complete, blob size:', blob.size, 'bytes');\n\n                // Verify blob is valid\n                if (blob.size === 0) {\n                  reject(new Error('Export failed: video blob is empty'));\n                  return;\n                }\n\n                resolve(blob);\n              } catch (error) {\n                hasErrored = true;\n                cleanup();\n                reject(error);\n              }\n            }, 1000); // Increased from 500ms to 1000ms for better reliability\n          }\n        } catch (error) {\n          hasErrored = true;\n          videoRecorder.cancel();\n          cleanup();\n          reject(error);\n        }\n      };\n\n      audioElement.onerror = () => {\n        hasErrored = true;\n        videoRecorder.cancel();\n        cleanup();\n        reject(new Error('Audio playback error'));\n      };\n\n      // Render first frame immediately to ensure recording starts with content\n      requestAnimationFrame(renderFrame);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get list of available built-in visualizers\n   */\n  static getAvailableVisualizers(): string[] {\n    return Object.keys(BUILT_IN_VISUALIZERS);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get supported output formats\n   */\n  static getSupportedFormats(): string[] {\n    return VideoRecorder.getSupportedFormats();\n {\n    96→    if (this.debug) {\n    97→      console.log('[OfflineAudioAnalyzer]', ...args);\n    98→    }\n    99→  }\n   100→\n   101→  /**\n   102→   * Get FFT size\n   103→   */\n   104→  get fftSize(): number {\n   105→    return this._fftSize;\n   106→  }\n   107→\n   108→  /**\n   109→   * Set FFT size (will require re-analysis)\n   110→   */\n   111→  set fftSize(value: number) {\n   112→    if (!this.isValidFftSize(value)) {\n   113→      throw new Error('FFT size must be a power of 2 between 32 and 32768');\n   114→    }\n   115→    this._fftSize = value;\n   116→    // Invalidate cache when FFT size changes\n   117→    this.cache = null;\n   118→  }\n   119→\n   120→  /**\n   121→   * Check if analysis is currently in progress\n   122→   */\n   123→  get {\n   138→    return this.cache !== null;\n   139→  }\n   140→\n   141→  /**\n   142→   * Get the duration of the cached audio (or 0 if not cached)\n   143→   */\n   144→  get duration(): number {\n   145→    return this.cache?.duration ?? 0;\n   146→  }\n   147→\n   148→  /**\n   149→   * Abort any in-progress analysis\n   150→   */\n   151→  abortAnalysis(): void {\n   152→    if (this.abortController) {\n   153→      this.abortController.abort();\n   154→      this.abortController = null;\n   155→    }\n   156→    this.analysisInProgress = false;\n   157→  }\n   158→\n   159→  /**\n   160→   * Analyze an audio file and cache the visualization data\n   161→   * @param source - File, URL string, or ArrayBuffer containing audio data\n   162→   * @param onProgress - Optional progress callback (0-1)\n   163→   * @returns Promise resolving to the analysis cache\n   164→   */\n   165→  async analyzeAudio(\n   166→    source: File | string | ArrayBuffer,\n   167→    onProgress?: AnalysisProgressCallback\n   168→  ): Promise<AudioAnalysisCache> {\n   169→    // Abort any previous analysis\n   170→    this.abortAnalysis();\n   171→\n   172→    this.analysisInProgress = true;\n   173→    this.abortController = new AbortController();\n   174→    const signal = this.abortController.signal;\n   175→\n   176→    try {\n   177→      this.log('Starting audio analysis...');\n   178→\n   179→      // Step 1: Load the audio data into an ArrayBuffer\n   180→      let arrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer;\n   181→      if (source instanceof ArrayBuffer) {\n   182→        arrayBuffer = source;\n   183→      } else if (source instanceof File) {\n   184→        arrayBuffer = await source.arrayBuffer();\n   185→      } else {\n   186→        // URL string\n   187→        const response = await fetch(source);\n   188→        if (!response.ok) {\n   189→          throw new Error(`Failed to fetch audio: ${response.statusText}`);\n   190→        }\n   191→        arrayBuffer = await response.arrayBuffer();\n   192→      }\n   193→\n   194→      if (signal.aborted) {\n   195→        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n   196→      }\n   197→\n   198→      onProgress?.(0.1);\n   199→      this.log('Audio data loaded, size:', arrayBuffer.byteLength, 'bytes');\n   200→\n   201→      // Step 2: Decode the audio using AudioContext\n   202→      const audioContext = new AudioContext();\n   203→      const audioBuffer = await audioContext.decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer.slice(0));\n   204→      await audioContext.close();\n   205→\n   206→      if (signal.aborted) {\n   207→        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n   208→      }\n   209→\n   210→      onProgress?.(0.2);\n   211→      this.log('Audio decoded:', {\n   212→ // Calculate optimal segment count (min 10, max maxSegments)\n   223→      // For a typical 3-minute song at 1000 segments, each segment is ~180ms\n   224→      const segmentCount = Math.min(\n   225→        this.maxSegments,\n   226→        Math.max(10, Math.min(this.targetSegments, Math.ceil(duration * 30))) // ~30 segments per second for smooth scrubbing\n   227→      );\n   228→\n   229→      const segmentDuration = duration / segmentCount;\n   230→      const samplesPerSegment = Math.floor(sampleRate * segmentDuration);\n   231→\n   232→      this.log('Analysis parameters:', {\n   233→        segmentCount,\n   234→        segmentDuration: segmentDuration.toFixed(3) + 's',\n   235→        samplesPerSegment,\n   236→      });\n   237→\n   238→      // Step 4: Pre-allocate result array\n   239→      const segments: CachedVisualizationData[] = new Array(segmentCount);\n   240→\n   241→      // Step 5: Extract channel data (use channel 0, or mix stereo to mono)\n   242→      let pcmData: Float32Array;\n   243→      if (audioBuffer.numberOfChannels === 1) {\n   244→        pcmData = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0);\n   245→      } else {\n   246→        // Mix stereo to mono\n   247→        const left = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0);\n   248→        const right = audioBuffer.getChannelData(1);\n   249→        pcmData = new Float32Array(left.length);\n   250→        for (let i = 0; i < left.length; i++) {\n   251→          pcmData[i] = (left[i] + right[i]) / 2;\n   252→        }\n   253→      }\n   254→\n   255→      if (signal.aborted) {\n   256→        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n   257→      }\n   258→\n   259→      onProgress?.(0.3);\n   260→\n   261→      // Step 6: Analyze each segment\n   262→      const frequencyBinCount = this._fftSize / 2;\n   263→\n   264→      for (let i = 0; i < segmentCount; i++) {\n   265→        if (signal.aborted) {\n   266→          throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n   267→        }\n   268→\n   269→        const startSample = Math.floor(i * samplesPerSegment);\n   270→        const endSample = Math.min(startSample + samplesPerSegment, pcmData.length);\n   271→\n   272→        segments[i] = this.analyzeSegment(\n   273→          pcmData,\n   274→          startSample,\n   275→          endSample,\n   276→          this._fftSize,\n   277→          frequencyBinCount,\n   278→          sampleRate\n   279→        );\n   280→\n   281→        // Report progress (20% for loading, 70% for analysis)\n   282→        if (i % 50 === 0 || i === segmentCount - 1) {\n   283→          const analysisProgress = 0.3 + (i / segmentCount) * 0.7;\n   284→          onProgress?.(analysisProgress);\n   285→        }\n   286→      }\n   287→\n   288→      // Step 7: Create and store the cache\n   289→      this.cache = {\n   290→        duration,\n   291→        sampleRate,\n   292→        segmentCount,\n   293→        segmentDuration,\n   294→        segments,\n   295→        audioBuffer,\n   296→        fftSize: this._fftSize,\n   297→      };\n   298→\n   299→      this.analysisInProgress = false;\n   300→      onProgress?.(1);\n   301→      this.log('Analysis complete:', this.cache.segmentCount, 'segments cached');\n   302→\n   303→      return this.cache;\n   304→    } catch (error) {\n   305→      this.analysisInProgress = false;\n   306→      if ((error as Error).message !== 'Analysis aborted') {\n   307→        this.log('Analysis failed:', error);\n   308→      }\n   309→      throw error;\n   310→    }\n   311→  }\n   312→\n   313→  /**\n   314→   * Analyze a single segment of audio data\n   315→   */\n   316→  private analyzeSegment(\n   317→    pcmData: Float32Array,\n   318→    startSample: number,\n   319→    endSample: number,\n   320→    fftSize: number,\n   321→    frequencyBinCount: number,\n   322→    sampleRate: number\n   323→  ): CachedVisualizationData {\n   324→    const segmentLength = endSample - startSample;\n   325→\n   326→    // Calculate peak and RMS amplitude\n   327→    let peak = 0;\n   328→    let sumSquares = 0;\n   329→\n   330→    for (let i = startSample; i < endSample; i++) {\n   331→      const value = Math.abs(pcmData[i]);\n   332→      peak = Math.max(peak, value);\n   333→      sumSquares += pcmData[i] * pcmData[i];\n   334→    }\n   335→\n   336→    const rms = Math.sqrt(sumSquares / segmentLength);\n   337→\n   338→    // Generate time domain data (downsampled waveform)\n   339→    // Match the format of AnalyserNode.getByteTimeDomainData (0-255, 128 = center)\n   340→    const timeDomainData = new Uint8Array(fftSize);\n   341→    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(segmentLength / fftSize));\n   342→\n   343→    for (let i = 0; i < fftSize; i++) {\n   344→      const sampleIndex = startSample + Math.min(i * step, segmentLength - 1);\n   345→      // Convert from -1..1 to 0..255 range (128 is center/silence)\n   346→      const value = pcmData[sampleIndex];\n   347→      timeDomainData[i] = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round((value + 1) * 127.5)));\n   348→    }\n   349→\n   350→    // Generate frequency data using simple DFT-like analysis\n   351→    // This is a simplified frequency analysis that's fast but approximate\n   352→    // For more accurate results, a proper FFT library could be used\n   353→    const frequencyData = this.computeFrequencyData(\n   354→      pcmData,\n   355→      startSample,\n   356→      endSample,\n   357→      frequencyBinCount,\n   358→      sampleRate\n   359→    );\n   360→\n   361→    return {\n   362→      timeDomainData,\n   363→      frequencyData,\n   364→      peakAmplitude: peak,\n   365→      rmsAmplitude: rms,\n   366→    };\n   367→  }\n   368→\n   369→  /**\n   370→   * Compute frequency data for a segment\n   371→   * Uses a simplified magnitude spectrum approach\n   372→   */\n   373→  private computeFrequencyData(\n   374→    pcmData: Float32Array,\n   375→    startSample: number,\n   376→    endSample: number,\n   377→    frequencyBinCount: number,\n   378→    _sampleRate: number\n   379→  ): Uint8Array {\n   380→    const frequencyData = new Uint8Array(frequencyBinCount);\n   381→    const segmentLength = endSample - startSample;\n   382→\n   383→    // Use bands-based analysis for efficiency\n   384→    // This approximates what you'd get from FFT but is much faster\n   385→    const bandsPerOctave = 3;\n   386→    const numBands = Math.min(frequencyBinCount, 64);\n   387→\n   388→    // Calculate energy in frequency bands using autocorrelation-like approach\n   389→    // This is a simplified but fast method\n   390→    for (let band = 0; band < numBands; band++) {\n   391→      // Map band to approximate frequency range\n   392→      // Lower bands = lower frequencies (longer periods)\n   393→      const periodFactor = Math.pow(2, (numBands - band - 1) / bandsPerOctave);\n   394→      const period = Math.max(2, Math.min(segmentLength / 2, Math.round(periodFactor * 4)));\n   395→\n   396→      let energy = 0;\n   397→      let count = 0;\n   398→\n   399→      // Calculate energy at this approximate period\n   400→      for (let i = startSample; i < endSample - period; i += period) {\n   401→        const diff = Math.abs(pcmData[i] - pcmData[i + period]);\n   402→        const product = pcmData[i] * pcmData[i + Math.floor(period / 2)];\n   403→        energy += Math.abs(product) - diff * 0.5;\n   404→        count++;\n   405→      }\n   406→\n   407→      if (count > 0) {\n   408→        energy = energy / count;\n   409→      }\n   410→\n   411→      // Scale and clamp to 0-255 range\n   412→      // Apply some boost to lower frequencies to match typical music visualization\n   413→      const boost = 1 + (numBands - band) / numBands;\n   414→      const scaledEnergy = Math.abs(energy) * 500 * boost;\n   415→\n   416→      // Map to the corresponding bins\n   417→      const binStart = Math.floor((band / numBands) * frequencyBinCount);\n   418→      const binEnd = Math.floor(((band + 1) / numBands) * frequencyBinCount);\n   419→\n   420→      for (let bin = binStart; bin < binEnd; bin++) {\n   421→        frequencyData[bin] = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round(scaledEnergy)));\n   422→      }\n   423→    }\n   424→\n   425→    return frequencyData;\n   426→  }\n   427→\n   428→  /**\n   429→   * Get visualization data for a specific time in the audio\n   430→   * Returns cached data if available, or interpolates between segments\n   431→   * @param time - Time in seconds\n   432→   * @returns Cached visualization data for the specified time, or null if not cached\n   433→   */\n   434→  getDataAtTime(time: number): CachedVisualizationData | null {\n   435→    if (!this.cache) {\n   436→      return null;\n   437→    }\n   438→\n   439→    // Clamp time to valid range\n   440→    const clampedTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(time, this.cache.duration));\n   441→\n   442→    // Find the segment index\n   443→    const segmentIndex = Math.floor(clampedTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n   444→    const clampedIndex = Math.min(segmentIndex, this.cache.segmentCount - 1);\n   445→\n   446→    return seconds\n   452→   * @param endTime - End time in seconds\n   453→   * @param maxPoints - Maximum number of points to return\n   454→   * @returns Array of cached visualization data\n   455→   */\n   456→  getDataInRange(\n   457→    startTime: number,\n   458→    endTime: number,\n   459→    maxPoints: number = 100\n   460→  ): CachedVisualizationData[] | null {\n   461→    if (!this.cache) {\n   462→      return null;\n   463→    }\n   464→\n   465→    const startIndex = Math.floor(startTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n   466→    const endIndex = Math.ceil(endTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n   467→\n   468→    const clampedStart = Math.max(0, startIndex);\n   469→    const clampedEnd = Math.min(this.cache.segmentCount, endIndex);\n   470→\n   471→    const totalSegments = clampedEnd - clampedStart;\n   472→    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(totalSegments / maxPoints));\n   473→\n   474→    const result: CachedVisualizationData[] = [];\n   475→    for (let i = clampedStart; i < clampedEnd; i += step) {\n   476→      result.push(this.cache.segments[i]);\n   477→    }\n   478→\n   479→    return result;\n   480→  }\n   481→\n   482→  /**\n   483→   * Get a waveform overview for the entire audio file\n   484→   * Returns peak amplitudes suitable for drawing a waveform preview\n   485→   * @param numPoints - Number of points to generate\n   486→   * @returns Array of peak amplitude values (0-1)\n   487→   */\n   488→  getWaveformOverview(numPoints: number = 200): Float32Array | null {\n   489→    if (!this.cache) {\n   490→      return [2026-01-11T02:21:02.718Z] [INFO] vanced use cases) */\n  audioBuffer: AudioBuffer;\n  /** Number of data points per segment for time domain data */\n  fftSize: number;\n}\n\n/**\n * Progress callback size for frequency analysis (power of 2, 32-32768) */\n  fftSize?: number;\n  /** Target number of segments to cache (affects analysis granularity) */\n  targetSegments?: number;\n  /** Maximum segments to prevent memory issues with very long files */\n  maxSegments?: number;\n  /** Enable verbose logging */\n  debug?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Offline Audio Analyzer class\n * Pre-analyzes this.debug = options.debug ?? false;\n\n    // Validate FFT size\n    if (!this.isValidFftSize(this._fftSize)) {\n      throw new Error('FFT size must be a power of 2 between 32 and 32768');\n    }\n  }\n\n  private isValidFftSize(size: number): boolean {\n    return size >= 32 && size <= 32768 && (size & (size - 1)) === 0;\n  }\n\n  private log(...args: unknown[]): void {\n    if (this.debug) {\n      console.log('[OfflineAudioAnalyzer]', ...args);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get FFT size\n   */\n  get fftSize(): number {\n    return this._fftSize;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set FFT size (will require value;\n    // Invalidate cache when FFT size changes\n    this.cache = null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if analysis is currently in progress\n   */\n  get isAnalyzing(): boolean {\n    return this.abortController.abort();\n      this.abortController = null;\n    }\n    this.analysisInProgress = false;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Analyze an audio file and cache the visualization data\n   * @param source - File, URL string, or ArrayBuffer containing audio data\n   * @param onProgress - Optional progress callback (0-1)\n   * @returns Promise resolving to the analysis cache\n   */\n  async analyzeAudio(\n    source: File | string | ArrayBuffer,\n    onProgress?: AnalysisProgressCallback\n  ): Promise<AudioAnalysisCache> {\n    // Abort any previous analysis\n    this.abortAnalysis();\n\n    this.analysisInProgress = true;\n    this.abortController = new AbortController();\n    const signal = this.abortController.signal;\n\n    try {\n      this.log('Starting audio analysis...');\n\n      // Step 1: Load the audio data into an ArrayBuffer\n      let arrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer;\n      if (source instanceof ArrayBuffer) {\n        arrayBuffer = source;\n      } else if (source instanceof File) {\n        arrayBuffer = await source.arrayBuffer();\n      } else {\n        // URL string\n        const response = await fetch(source);\n        if (!response.ok) {\n          throw new Error(`Failed to fetch audio: ${response.statusText}`);\n        }\n        arrayBuffer = await response.arrayBuffer();\n      }\n\n      if (signal.aborted) {\n        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n      }\n\n      onProgress?.(0.1);\n      this.log('Audio data loaded, size:', arrayBuffer.byteLength, 'bytes');\n\n      // Step 2: Decode the audio using AudioContext\n      const audioContext = new AudioContext();\n      const audioBuffer = await audioContext.decodeAudioData(arrayBuffer.slice(0));\n      await audioContext.close();\n\n      if (signal.aborted) {\n        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n      }\n\n      onProgress?.(0.2);\n      this.log('Audio decoded:', {\n        duration: audioBuffer.duration,\n        sampleRate: audioBuffer.sampleRate,\n        channels: audioBuffer.numberOfChannels,\n        length: audioBuffer.length,\n      });\n\n      // Step 3: Calculate segment parameters\n      const duration = audioBuffer.duration;\n      const sampleRate = audioBuffer.sampleRate;\n\n      // Calculate optimal segment count (min 10, max maxSegments)\n      // For a typical 3-minute song at 1000 segments, each segment is ~180ms\n      const segmentCount = Math.min(\n        this.maxSegments,\n        Math.max(10, Math.min(this.targetSegments, Math.ceil(duration * 30))) // ~30 segments per second for smooth scrubbing\n      );\n\n      const segmentDuration = duration / segmentCount;\n      const samplesPerSegment = Math.floor(sampleRate * segmentDuration);\n\n      this.log('Analysis parameters:', {\n        segmentCount,\n        segmentDuration: segmentDuration.toFixed(3) + 's',\n        samplesPerSegment,\n      });\n\n      // Step 4: Pre-allocate result array\n      const segments: CachedVisualizationData[] = new Array(segmentCount);\n\n      // Step 5: Extract channel data (use channel 0, or mix stereo to mono)\n      let pcmData: Float32Array;\n      if (audioBuffer.numberOfChannels === 1) {\n        pcmData = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0);\n      } else {\n        // Mix stereo to mono\n        const left = audioBuffer.getChannelData(0);\n        const right = audioBuffer.getChannelData(1);\n        pcmData = new Float32Array(left.length);\n        for (let i = 0; i < left.length; i++) {\n          pcmData[i] = (left[i] + right[i]) / 2;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (signal.aborted) {\n        throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n      }\n\n      onProgress?.(0.3);\n\n      // Step 6: Analyze each segment\n      const frequencyBinCount = this._fftSize / 2;\n\n      for (let i = 0; i < segmentCount; i++) {\n        if (signal.aborted) {\n          throw new Error('Analysis aborted');\n        }\n\n        const startSample = Math.floor(i * samplesPerSegment);\n        const endSample = Math.min(startSample + samplesPerSegment, pcmData.length);\n\n        segments[i] = this.analyzeSegment(\n          pcmData,\n          startSample,\n          endSample,\n          this._fftSize,\n          frequencyBinCount,\n          sampleRate\n        );\n\n        // Report progress (20% for loading, 70% for analysis)\n        if (i % 50 === 0 || i === segmentCount - 1) {\n          const analysisProgress = 0.3 + (i / segmentCount) * 0.7;\n          onProgress?.(analysisProgress);\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Step 7: Create and store the cache\n      this.cache = {\n        duration,\n        sampleRate,\n        segmentCount,\n        segmentDuration,\n        segments,\n        audioBuffer,\n        fftSize: this._fftSize,\n      };\n\n      this.analysisInProgress = false;\n      onProgress?.(1);\n      this.log('Analysis complete:', this.cache.segmentCount, 'segments cached');\n\n      return this.cache;\n    } catch (error) {\n      this.analysisInProgress = false;\n      if ((error as Error).message !== 'Analysis aborted') {\n        this.log('Analysis failed:', error);\n      }\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Analyze a single segment of audio data\n   */\n  private analyzeSegment(\n    pcmData: Float32Array,\n    startSample: number,\n    endSample: number,\n    fftSize: number,\n    frequencyBinCount: number,\n    sampleRate: number\n  ): CachedVisualizationData {\n    const segmentLength = endSample - startSample;\n\n    // Calculate peak and RMS amplitude\n    let peak = 0;\n    let sumSquares = 0;\n\n    for (let i = startSample; i < endSample; i++) {\n      const value = Math.abs(pcmData[i]);\n      peak = Math.max(peak, value);\n      sumSquares += pcmData[i] * pcmData[i];\n    }\n\n    const rms = Math.sqrt(sumSquares / segmentLength);\n\n    // Generate time domain data (downsampled waveform)\n    // Match the format of AnalyserNode.getByteTimeDomainData (0-255, 128 = center)\n    const timeDomainData = new Uint8Array(fftSize);\n    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(segmentLength / fftSize));\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < fftSize; i++) {\n      const sampleIndex = startSample + Math.min(i * step, segmentLength - 1);\n      // Convert from -1..1 to 0..255 range (128 is center/silence)\n      const value = pcmData[sampleIndex];\n      timeDomainData[i] = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round((value + 1) * 127.5)));\n    }\n\n    // Generate frequency data using simple DFT-like analysis\n    // This is a simplified frequency analysis that's fast but approximate\n    // For more accurate results, a proper FFT library could be used\n    const frequencyData = this.computeFrequencyData(\n      pcmData,\n      startSample,\n      endSample,\n      frequencyBinCount,\n      sampleRate\n    );\n\n    return {\n      timeDomainData,\n      frequencyData,\n      peakAmplitude: peak,\n      rmsAmplitude: rms,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Compute frequency data for a segment\n   * Uses a simplified magnitude spectrum approach\n   */\n  private computeFrequencyData(\n    pcmData: Float32Array,\n    startSample: number,\n    endSample: number,\n    frequencyBinCount: number,\n    _sampleRate: number\n  ): Uint8Array {\n    const frequencyData = new Uint8Array(frequencyBinCount);\n    const segmentLength = endSample - startSample;\n\n    // Use bands-based analysis for efficiency\n    // This approximates what you'd get from FFT but is much faster\n    const bandsPerOctave = 3;\n    const numBands = Math.min(frequencyBinCount, 64);\n\n    // Calculate energy in frequency bands using autocorrelation-like approach\n    // This is a simplified but fast method\n    for (let band = 0; band < numBands; band++) {\n      // Map band to approximate frequency range\n      // Lower bands = lower frequencies (longer periods)\n      const periodFactor = Math.pow(2, (numBands - band - 1) / bandsPerOctave);\n      const period = Math.max(2, Math.min(segmentLength / 2, Math.round(periodFactor * 4)));\n\n      let energy = 0;\n      let count = 0;\n\n      // Calculate energy at this approximate period\n      for (let i = startSample; i < endSample - period; i += period) {\n        const diff = Math.abs(pcmData[i] - pcmData[i + period]);\n        const product = pcmData[i] * pcmData[i + Math.floor(period / 2)];\n        energy += Math.abs(product) - diff * 0.5;\n        count++;\n      }\n\n      if (count > 0) {\n        energy = energy / count;\n      }\n\n      // Scale and clamp to 0-255 range\n      // Apply some boost to lower frequencies to match typical music visualization\n      const boost = 1 + (numBands - band) / numBands;\n      const scaledEnergy = Math.abs(energy) * 500 * boost;\n\n      // Map to the corresponding bins\n      const binStart = Math.floor((band / numBands) * frequencyBinCount);\n      const binEnd = Math.floor(((band + 1) / numBands) * frequencyBinCount);\n\n      for (let bin = binStart; bin < binEnd; bin++) {\n        frequencyData[bin] = Math.max(0, Math.min(255, Math.round(scaledEnergy)));\n      }\n    }\n\n    return frequencyData;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get visualization data for a specific time in the audio\n   * Returns cached data if available, or interpolates between segments\n   * @param time - Time in seconds\n   * @returns Cached visualization data for the specified time, or null if not cached\n   */\n  getDataAtTime(time: number): CachedVisualizationData | null {\n    if (!this.cache) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    // Clamp time to valid range\n    const clampedTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(time, this.cache.duration));\n\n    // Find the segment index\n    const segmentIndex = Math.floor(clampedTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n    const clampedIndex = Math.min(segmentIndex, this.cache.segmentCount - 1);\n\n    return this.cache.segments[clampedIndex];\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get visualization data for a time range (useful for waveform overview)\n   * @param startTime - Start time in seconds\n   * @param endTime - End time in seconds\n   * @param maxPoints - Maximum number of points to return\n   * @returns Array of cached visualization data\n   */\n  getDataInRange(\n    startTime: number,\n    endTime: number,\n    maxPoints: number = 100\n  ): CachedVisualizationData[] | null {\n    if (!this.cache) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const startIndex = Math.floor(startTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n    const endIndex = Math.ceil(endTime / this.cache.segmentDuration);\n\n    const clampedStart = Math.max(0, startIndex);\n    const clampedEnd = Math.min(this.cache.segmentCount, endIndex);\n\n    const totalSegments = clampedEnd - clampedStart;\n    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(totalSegments / maxPoints));\n\n    const result: CachedVisualizationData[] = [];\n    for (let i = clampedStart; i < clampedEnd; i += step) {\n      result.push(this.cache.segments[i]);\n    }\n\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a waveform overview for the entire audio file\n   * Returns peak amplitudes suitable for drawing a waveform preview\n   * @param numPoints - Number of points to generate\n   * @returns Array of peak amplitude values (0-1)\n   */\n  getWaveformOverview(numPoints: number = 200): Float32Array | null {\n    if (!this.cache) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const result = new Float32Array(numPoints);\n    const step = this.cache.segmentCount / numPoints;\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < numPoints; i++) {\n      const segmentIndex = Math.min(\n        Math.floor(i * step),\n        this.cache.segmentCount - 1\n      );\n      result[i] = this.cache.segments[segmentIndex].peakAmplitude;\n    }\n\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clear the cached analysis data\n   */\n  clearCache(): void {\n    this.cache = null;\n    this.log('Cache cleared');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clean up all resources\n   */\n  destroy(): void {\n    this.abortAnalysis();\n    this.clearCache();\n    this.log('Destroyed');\n  }\n}\n","numLines":524,"startLine":1,"totalLines":524}}}

30;\n\n    // Ensure canvas is using proper color settings for export\n    // This helps prevent color shifts in the exported video\n    const canvasStream = canvas.captureStream(fps);\n    this.log('Got canvas stream at', fps, 'fps');\n\n    // Combine canvas and audio streams\n    const tracks = [...canvasStream.getTracks()];\n    if (audioStream) {\n      tracks.push(...audioStream.getAudioTracks());\n      this.log('Added', audioStream.getAudioTracks().length, 'audio tracks');\n    }\n    this.stream = new MediaStream(tracks);\n\n    // Create MediaRecorder with higher quality settings\n    // Increased default bitrate from 2.5Mbps to 8Mbps for better quality\n    const recorderOptions: MediaRecorderOptions = {\n      mimeType,\n      videoBitsPerSecond: options.videoBitrate ?? 8000000,\n    };\n    if (audioStream && options.audioBitrate) {\n      recorderOptions.audioBitsPerSecond = options.audioBitrate;\n    }\n\n    this.mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(this.stream, recorderOptions);\n    this.recordedChunks = [];\n\n    this.mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {\n      if (event.data.size > 0) {\n        this.recordedChunks.push(event.data);\n        this.log('Received chunk:', event.data.size, 'bytes');\n      }\n    };\n\n    this.mediaRecorder.onerror = (event) => {\n      console.error('[VideoRecorder] Error:', event);\n    };\n\n    // Request data every second for better memory management\n    this.mediaRecorder.start(1000);\n    this._state = 'recording';\n    this.log('Started recording with mimeType:', mimeType);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Pause recording\n   */\n  pause(): void {\n    if (this._state !== 'recording' || !this.mediaRecorder) {\n      throw new Error('Cannot pause: not recording');\n    }\n    this.mediaRecorder.pause();\n    this._state = 'paused';\n    this.log('Paused recording');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Resume recording\n   */\n  resume(): void {\n    if (this._state !== 'paused' || !this.mediaRecorder) {\n      throw new Error('Cannot resume: not paused');\n    }\n    this.mediaRecorder.resume();\n    this._state = 'recording';\n    this.log('Resumed recording');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Stop recording and return the recorded blob\n   */\n  async stop(): Promise<Blob> {\n    if (this._state === 'inactive' || !this.mediaRecorder) {\n      throw new Error('Cannot stop: not recording');\n    }\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      if (!this.mediaRecorder) {\n        reject(new Error('MediaRecorder not initialized'));\n        return;\n      }\n\n      this.mediaRecorder.onstop = () => {\n        const mimeType = this.mediaRecorder?.mimeType ?? 'video/webm';\n        const blob = new Blob(this.recordedChunks, { type: mimeType });\n        this.log('Recording stopped, total size:', blob.size, 'bytes');\n\n        // Clean up\n        this.cleanup();\n\n        resolve(blob);\n      };\n\n      this.mediaRecorder.stop();\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clean up resources\n   */\n  private cleanup(): void {\n    if (this.stream) {\n      // Only stop video tracks (canvas stream), NOT audio tracks\n      // Audio tracks belong to the original microphone stream and should remain active\n      // for subsequent recordings\n      this.stream.getVideoTracks().forEach((track) => track.stop());\n      this.stream = null;\n    }\n    this.mediaRecorder = null;\n    this.recordedChunks = [];\n    this._state = 'inactive';\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Cancel recording and discard data\n   */\n  cancel(): void {\n    if (this._state === 'inactive') {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (this.mediaRecorder && this.mediaRecorder.state !== 'inactive') {\n      this.mediaRecorder.stop();\n    }\n    this.cleanup();\n    this.log('Recording cancelled');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get current recording state\n   */\n  get state(): RecordingState {\n    return this._state;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get current recording duration in milliseconds\n   */\n  get recordedSize(): number {\n    return this.recordedChunks.reduce((total, chunk) => total + chunk.size, 0);\n  }\n}\n",
      "numLines": 228,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 228
    }
  }

          "new_string": "      // Render first frame immediately to ensure recording starts with content\n      requestAnimationFrame(renderFrame);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Convert an audio file to video with visualization using offline rendering\n   * This renders frames as fast as possible without real-time playback,\n   * making conversion much faster than real-time (typically 5-20x faster).\n   * @param config - Conversion configuration\n   * @returns Promise resolving to the video blob\n   */\n  async convertOffline(config: ConversionConfig): Promise<Blob> {\n    // Reset cancellation flag\n    this.isCancelled = false;\n\n    const {\n      audioSource,\n      canvas: canvasConfig,\n      visualizer: visualizerConfig,\n      visualizerOptions,\n      fps = 30,\n      videoWidth = 1920,\n      videoHeight = 1080,\n      videoBitrate = 8000000,\n      audioBitrate = 192000,\n      format = 'webm',\n      onProgress,\n    } = config;\n\n    // Get canvas element\n    let canvas: HTMLCanvasElement;\n    if (typeof canvasConfig === 'string') {\n      const element = document.querySelector(canvasConfig);\n      if (!element || !(element instanceof HTMLCanvasElement)) {\n        throw new Error(`Canvas element not found: ${canvasConfig}`);\n      }\n      canvas = element;\n    } else {\n      canvas = canvasConfig;\n    }\n\n    // Set canvas size\n    canvas.width = videoWidth;\n    canvas.height = videoHeight;\n\n    // Get 2D context with color space settings for better color accuracy\n    const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d', {\n      alpha: true,\n      colorSpace: 'srgb',\n      willReadFrequently: false,\n    });\n    if (!ctx) {\n      throw new Error('Failed to get 2D context from canvas');\n    }\n\n    // Set image smoothing for better quality\n    ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = true;\n    ctx.imageSmoothingQuality = 'high';\n\n    // Create visualizer\n    let visualizer: Visualizer;\n    if (visualizerConfig) {\n      if (typeof visualizerConfig === 'string') {\n        visualizer = this.createBuiltInVisualizer(visualizerConfig, visualizerOptions);\n      } else {\n        visualizer = visualizerConfig;\n      }\n    } else {\n      visualizer = new BarVisualizer(visualizerOptions);\n    }\n    // Wait for visualizer initialization (including image loading) to prevent flickering\n    await visualizer.init(canvas, visualizerOptions);\n\n    this.log('Starting offline conversion...');\n\n    // Create offline analyzer and analyze the audio\n    const offlineAnalyzer = new OfflineAudioAnalyzer({\n      fftSize: 2048,\n      debug: this.debug,\n    });\n\n    // Report analysis progress (0-30% of total)\n    const analysisProgress = (progress: number): void => {\n      if (onProgress) {\n        onProgress(progress * 0.3);\n      }\n    };\n\n    let arrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer;\n    if (audioSource instanceof File) {\n      arrayBuffer = await audioSource.arrayBuffer();\n    } else {\n      const response = await fetch(audioSource);\n      if (!response.ok) {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to fetch audio: ${response.statusText}`);\n      }\n      arrayBuffer = await response.arrayBuffer();\n    }\n\n    // Keep a copy of the original audio data for later\n    const originalArrayBuffer = arrayBuffer.slice(0);\n\n    // Analyze the audio to get visualization data\n    const analysisCache = await offlineAnalyzer.analyzeAudio(arrayBuffer, analysisProgress);\n    this.log('Audio analysis complete:', analysisCache.segmentCount, 'segments');\n\n    if (this.isCancelled) {\n      visualizer.destroy();\n      offlineAnalyzer.destroy();\n      throw new Error('Conversion cancelled by user');\n    }\n\n    // Calculate total frames needed\n    const duration = analysisCache.duration;\n    const totalFrames = Math.ceil(duration * fps);\n    const frameDuration = 1000 / fps; // in milliseconds\n\n    this.log('Rendering', totalFrames, 'frames at', fps, 'fps');\n\n    // Create video recorder\n    const videoRecorder = new VideoRecorder({ debug: this.debug });\n\n    // For offline rendering, we'll render all frames first, then combine with audio\n    // Start recording from canvas (we'll add audio afterwards)\n    videoRecorder.start(canvas, undefined, {\n      format,\n      fps,\n      videoBitrate,\n      audioBitrate,\n    });\n\n    // Render frames as fast as possible\n    const startTime = performance.now();\n    let frameCount = 0;\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      let hasErrored = false;\n\n      const cleanup = (): void => {\n        visualizer.destroy();\n        offlineAnalyzer.destroy();\n      };\n\n      const renderNextFrame = (): void => {\n        if (hasErrored) return;\n\n        // Check for cancellation\n        if (this.isCancelled) {\n          hasErrored = true;\n          videoRecorder.cancel();\n          cleanup();\n          reject(new Error('Conversion cancelled by user'));\n          return;\n        }\n\n        try {\n          // Calculate current time in the audio\n          const currentTime = frameCount / fps;\n\n          // Get visualization data from cache\n          const cachedData = offlineAnalyzer.getDataAtTime(currentTime);\n\n          if (cachedData) {\n            const data: VisualizationData = {\n              timeDomainData: cachedData.timeDomainData,\n              frequencyData: cachedData.frequencyData,\n              timestamp: frameCount * frameDuration,\n              width: canvas.width,\n              height: canvas.height,\n              sampleRate: analysisCache.sampleRate,\n              fftSize: analysisCache.fftSize,\n            };\n\n            visualizer.draw(ctx, data);\n          }\n\n          frameCount++;\n\n          // Report progress (30-95% for rendering)\n          if (onProgress) {\n            const renderProgress = 0.3 + (frameCount / totalFrames) * 0.65;\n            onProgress(Math.min(renderProgress, 0.95));\n          }\n\n          // Continue rendering or finish\n          if (frameCount < totalFrames) {\n            // Use setImmediate-like scheduling for maximum speed\n            // setTimeout with 0 allows the browser to process other tasks\n            setTimeout(renderNextFrame, 0);\n          } else {\n            // All frames rendered, finalize recording\n            this.log('Rendering complete, rendered', frameCount, 'frames in',\n              ((performance.now() - startTime) / 1000).toFixed(2), 'seconds');\n\n            // Wait a bit for the last frames to be captured\n            setTimeout(async () => {\n              if (hasErrored) return;\n\n              try {\n                const videoOnlyBlob = await videoRecorder.stop();\n                this.log('Video recording stopped, blob size:', videoOnlyBlob.size, 'bytes');\n\n                // Now we need to mux the audio with the video\n                // For WebM, we can use a simple approach by creating an audio+video output\n                const finalBlob = await this.addAudioToVideo(\n                  videoOnlyBlob,\n                  originalArrayBuffer,\n                  format,\n                  audioBitrate\n                );\n\n                cleanup();\n\n                if (onProgress) {\n                  onProgress(1);\n                }\n\n                this.log('Offline conversion complete, final size:', finalBlob.size, 'bytes');\n\n                if (finalBlob.size === 0) {\n                  reject(new Error('Export failed: video blob is empty'));\n                  return;\n                }\n\n                resolve(finalBlob);\n              } catch (error) {\n                hasErrored = true;\n                cleanup();\n                reject(error);\n              }\n            }, 500);\n          }\n        } catch (error) {\n          hasErrored = true;\n          videoRecorder.cancel();\n          cleanup();\n          reject(error);\n        }\n      };\n\n      // Start rendering\n      renderNextFrame();\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Add audio track to a video-only blob\n   * This creates a new recording that combines the video with the original audio\n   */\n  private async addAudioToVideo(\n    videoBlob: Blob,\n    audioArrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer,\n    format: 'webm' | 'mp4',\n    audioBitrate: number\n  ): Promise<Blob> {\n    this.log('Adding audio to video...');\n\n    // Decode the audio\n    const audioContext = new AudioContext();\n    const audioBuffer = await audioContext.decodeAudioData(audioArrayBuffer.slice(0));\n    const duration = audioBuffer.duration;\n\n    // Create an offline audio context to render the audio\n    const offlineContext = new OfflineAudioContext(\n      audioBuffer.numberOfChannels,\n      Math.ceil(audioBuffer.sampleRate * duration),\n      audioBuffer.sampleRate\n    );\n\n    const source = offlineContext.createBufferSource();\n    source.buffer = audioBuffer;\n    source.connect(offlineContext.destination);\n    source.start(0);\n\n    // Render the audio\n    const renderedBuffer = await offlineContext.startRendering();\n    await audioContext.close();\n\n    // Convert rendered buffer to audio blob\n    const audioBlob = await this.audioBufferToBlob(renderedBuffer, format === 'webm' ? 'audio/webm' : 'audio/mp4');\n\n    // Create a video element to get the video stream\n    const videoUrl = URL.createObjectURL(videoBlob);\n    const videoElement = document.createElement('video');\n    videoElement.src = videoUrl;\n    videoElement.muted = true;\n\n    // Create an audio element to get the audio stream\n    const audioUrl = URL.createObjectURL(audioBlob);\n    const audioElement = document.createElement('audio');\n    audioElement.src = audioUrl;\n\n    // Wait for both to be ready\n    await Promise.all([\n      new Promise<void>((resolve) => {\n        videoElement.onloadedmetadata = () => resolve();\n      }),\n      new Promise<void>((resolve) => {\n        audioElement.onloadedmetadata = () => resolve();\n      }),\n    ]);\n\n    // Get streams from both elements\n    let videoStream: MediaStream | null = null;\n    let audioStream: MediaStream | null = null;\n\n    try {\n      if ('captureStream' in videoElement) {\n        videoStream = (videoElement as HTMLVideoElement & { captureStream(): MediaStream }).captureStream();\n      } else if ('mozCaptureStream' in videoElement) {\n        videoStream = (videoElement as HTMLVideoElement & { mozCaptureStream(): MediaStream }).mozCaptureStream();\n      }\n    } catch (e) {\n      this.log('Could not capture video stream:', e);\n    }\n\n    try {\n      if ('captureStream' in audioElement) {\n        audioStream = (audioElement as HTMLAudioElement & { captureStream(): MediaStream }).captureStream();\n      } else if ('mozCaptureStream' in audioElement) {\n        audioStream = (audioElement as HTMLAudioElement & { mozCaptureStream(): MediaStream }).mozCaptureStream();\n      }\n    } catch (e) {\n      this.log('Could not capture audio stream:', e);\n    }\n\n    // If we can't capture streams, return video without audio\n    if (!videoStream || !audioStream) {\n      this.log('Could not capture streams, returning video without audio');\n      URL.revokeObjectURL(videoUrl);\n      URL.revokeObjectURL(audioUrl);\n      return videoBlob;\n    }\n\n    // Combine streams\n    const combinedTracks = [\n      ...videoStream.getVideoTracks(),\n      ...audioStream.getAudioTracks(),\n    ];\n    const combinedStream = new MediaStream(combinedTracks);\n\n    // Get MIME type\n    const mimeType = VideoRecorder.getSupportedMimeType(format);\n    if (!mimeType) {\n      throw new Error(`Format \"${format}\" is not supported`);\n    }\n\n    // Record the combined stream\n    const mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(combinedStream, {\n      mimeType,\n      audioBitsPerSecond: audioBitrate,\n    });\n\n    const chunks: Blob[] = [];\n    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (e) => {\n      if (e.data.size > 0) {\n        chunks.push(e.data);\n      }\n    };\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      mediaRecorder.onstop = () => {\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(videoUrl);\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(audioUrl);\n        const finalBlob = new Blob(chunks, { type: mimeType ?? undefined });\n        this.log('Combined audio+video, final size:', finalBlob.size);\n        resolve(finalBlob);\n      };\n\n      mediaRecorder.onerror = (e) => {\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(videoUrl);\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(audioUrl);\n        reject(new Error('MediaRecorder error: ' + e));\n      };\n\n      // Start recording and playback\n      mediaRecorder.start();\n      videoElement.play();\n      audioElement.play();\n\n      // Stop when video ends\n      videoElement.onended = () => {\n        mediaRecorder.stop();\n      };\n\n      // Safety timeout\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        if (mediaRecorder.state !== 'inactive') {\n          mediaRecorder.stop();\n        }\n      }, (duration + 2) * 1000);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Convert an AudioBuffer to a Blob using MediaRecorder\n   */\n  private async audioBufferToBlob(audioBuffer: AudioBuffer, mimeType: string): Promise<Blob> {\n    const audioContext = new AudioContext();\n    const destination = audioContext.createMediaStreamDestination();\n    const source = audioContext.createBufferSource();\n    source.buffer = audioBuffer;\n    source.connect(destination);\n\n    const mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(destination.stream, {\n      mimeType: mimeType.includes('webm') ? 'audio/webm' : 'audio/mp4',\n    });\n\n    const chunks: Blob[] = [];\n    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (e) => {\n      if (e.data.size > 0) {\n        chunks.push(e.data);\n      }\n    };\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {\n        await audioContext.close();\n        resolve(new Blob(chunks, { type: mimeType }));\n      };\n\n      mediaRecorder.onerror = async (e) => {\n        await audioContext.close();\n        reject(new Error('Audio recording error: ' + e));\n      };\n\n      mediaRecorder.start();\n      source.start(0);\n\n      source.onended = () => {\n        // Give a small delay for final data\n        setTimeout(() => {\n          mediaRecorder.stop();\n        }, 100);\n      };\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get list of available built-in visualizers\n   */\n  static getAvailableVisualizers(): string[] {\n    return Object.keys(BUILT_IN_VISUALIZERS);\n  }"

*/\n  async convertOffline(config: ConversionConfig): Promise<Blob> {\n    // Reset cancellation flag\n    this.isCancelled = false;\n\n    const {\n      audioSource,\n      canvas: canvasConfig,\n  // Start recording from canvas (we'll add audio afterwards)\n    videoRecorder.start(canvas, undefined, {\n      format,\n      fps,\n      videoBitrate,\n      audioBitrate,\n    });\n\n    // Render frames as fast as possible\n    const startTime = performance.now();\n    let frameCount = 0;\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      let hasErrored = false;\n\n      const     const cachedData = offlineAnalyzer.getDataAtTime(currentTime);\n\n          if (cachedData) {\n            const data: VisualizationData = {\n              timeDomainData: canvas.height,\n              sampleRate: analysisCache.sampleRate,\n              fftSize: analysisCache.fftSize,\n            };\n\n            visualizer.draw(ctx, data);\n          }\n\n          frameCount++;\n\n          // Report progress (30-95% for rendering)\n          if (onProgress) {\n            const renderProgress = 0.3 + (frameCount / totalFrames) * 0.65;\n            onProgress(Math.min(renderProgress, 0.95));\n          }\n\n          // Continue rendering or finish\n          if (frameCount < totalFrames) {\n            // Use setImmediate-like scheduling for originalArrayBuffer,\n                  format,\n                  audioBitrate\n                );\n\n                cleanup();\n\n                if (onProgress) {\n                  onProgress(1);\n                }\n\n                this.log('Offline conversion complete, final size:', finalBlob.size, 'bytes');\n\n                if (finalBlob.size === 0) {\n                  reject(new Error('Export failed: video blob is empty'));\n                  return;\n                }\n\n                resolve(finalBlob);\n              } catch (error) {\n                hasErrored = true;\n                cleanup();\n                reject(error);\n              }\n            }, 500);\n          }\n        } catch (error) {\n          hasErrored = true;\n          videoRecorder.cancel();\n          cleanup();\n          reject(error);\n        }\n      };\n\n      // Start rendering\n      renderNextFrame();\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Add audio track to a video-only blob\n   * This creates a new recording that combines the video with the original audio\n   */\n  private async addAudioToVideo(\n    videoBlob: Blob,\n    audioArrayBuffer: ArrayBuffer,\n    format: 'webm' | 'mp4',\n    audioBitrate: number\n  ): Promise<Blob> {\n    this.log('Adding audio to video...');\n\n    // Decode the audio\n    const audioContext = new AudioContext();\n    const audioBuffer = await audioContext.decodeAudioData(audioArrayBuffer.slice(0));\n    const duration = audioBuffer.duration;\n\n    // Create an offline audio context to render the audio\n    const offlineContext = new OfflineAudioContext(\n      audioBuffer.numberOfChannels,\n      Math.ceil(audioBuffer.sampleRate * duration),\n      audioBuffer.sampleRate\n    );\n\n    const source = offlineContext.createBufferSource();\n    source.buffer = audioBuffer;\n    source.connect(offlineContext.destination);\n    source.start(0);\n\n    // Render the audio\n    const renderedBuffer = await offlineContext.startRendering();\n    await audioContext.close();\n\n    // Convert rendered buffer to audio blob\n    const audioBlob = await this.audioBufferToBlob(renderedBuffer, format === 'webm' ? 'audio/webm' : 'audio/mp4');\n\n    // Create a video element to get the video stream\n    const videoUrl = URL.createObjectURL(videoBlob);\n    const videoElement = document.createElement('video');\n    videoElement.src = videoUrl;\n    videoElement.muted = true;\n\n    // Create an audio element to get the audio stream\n    const audioUrl = URL.createObjectURL(audioBlob);\n    const audioElement = document.createElement('audio');\n    audioElement.src = audioUrl;\n\n    // Wait for both to be ready\n    await Promise.all([\n      new Promise<void>((resolve) => {\n        videoElement.onloadedmetadata = () => resolve();\n      }),\n      new Promise<void>((resolve) => {\n        audioElement.onloadedmetadata = () => resolve();\n      }),\n    ]);\n\n    // Get streams from both elements\n    let videoStream: MediaStream | null = null;\n    let audioStream: MediaStream | null = null;\n\n    try {\n      if ('captureStream' in videoElement) {\n        videoStream = (videoElement as HTMLVideoElement & { captureStream(): MediaStream }).captureStream();\n      } else if ('mozCaptureStream' in videoElement) {\n        videoStream = (videoElement as HTMLVideoElement & { mozCaptureStream(): MediaStream }).mozCaptureStream();\n      }\n    } catch (e) {\n      this.log('Could not capture video stream:', e);\n    }\n\n    try {\n      if ('captureStream' in audioElement) {\n        audioStream = (audioElement as HTMLAudioElement & { captureStream(): MediaStream }).captureStream();\n      } else if ('mozCaptureStream' in audioElement) {\n        audioStream = (audioElement as HTMLAudioElement & { mozCaptureStream(): MediaStream }).mozCaptureStream();\n      }\n    } catch (e) {\n      this.log('Could not capture audio stream:', e);\n    }\n\n URL.revokeObjectURL(videoUrl);\n      URL.revokeObjectURL(audioUrl);\n      return videoBlob;\n    }\n\n    // Combine streams\n    const combinedTracks = [\n      ...videoStream.getVideoTracks(),\n      ...audioStream.getAudioTracks(),\n    ];\n    const combinedStream = new MediaStream(combinedTracks);\n\n    // Get MIME type\n    const mimeType = VideoRecorder.getSupportedMimeType(format);\n mimeType,\n      audioBitsPerSecond: audioBitrate,\n    });\n\n    const chunks: Blob[] = [];\n    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (e) => {\n      if (e.data.size > 0) {\n        chunks.push(e.data);\n      }\n    };\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      mediaRecorder.onstop = () => {\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(videoUrl);\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(audioUrl);\n        const finalBlob = new Blob(chunks, { type: mimeType ?? undefined });\n        this.log('Combined audio+video, final size:', finalBlob.size);\n Let me think of a better approach.\n\nActually, looking at this more carefully, I realize the issue: the `addAudioToVideo` method still requires playing the video and audio in real-time to record them together. This defeats the purpose of fast offline conversion.\n\nA better approach for true offline rendering would be:\n1. Render all frames and encode them as video (without audio) - but MediaRecorder doesn't support faster-than-realtime capture\n2. */\n  cancel(): void {\n    this.isCancelled = true;\n    this.log('Conversion cancelled by user');\n  }\n\n  private log(...args: unknown[]): void {\n    if (this.debug) {\n      console.log('[AudioToVideoConverter]', ...args);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Create a built-in visualizer by name\n   */\n  private createBuiltInVisualizer(\n    name: string,\n    options?: VisualizerOptions\n  ): Visualizer {\n    const VisualizerClass = BUILT_IN_VISUALIZERS[name];\n    if (!VisualizerClass) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Unknown visualizer: ${name}. Available: ${Object.keys(BUILT_IN_VISUALIZERS).join(', ')}`\n      );\n    }\n    return new VisualizerClass(options);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Convert an audio file to video with visualization\n   * @param config - Conversion configuration\n   * @returns Promise resolving to the video blob\n   */\n  async convert(config: ConversionConfig): Promise<Blob> {\n    // Reset cancellation flag\n    this.isCancelled = false;\n\n    const {\n      audioSource,\n      canvas: canvasConfig,\n      visualizer: visualizerConfig,\n      visualizerOptions,\n      fps = 30,\n      videoWidth = 1920,\n      videoHeight = 1080,\n      videoBitrate = 8000000,\n      audioBitrate = 192000,\n      format = 'webm',\n      onProgress,\n    } = config;\n\n    // Get canvas element\n    let canvas: throw new Error(`Canvas element not found: ${canvasConfig}`);\n      }\n      canvas = element;\n    } else {\n      canvas = canvasConfig;\n    }\n\n    // Set canvas size\n    canvas.width = }\n    } catch (e) {\n      this.log('Could not capture stream from audio element, video will have no audio');\n    }\n\n    // Start recording\n    videoRecorder.start(canvas, audioStream, {\n improved timing and reliability\n    const frameInterval = 1000 / fps;\n    let lastFrameTime = 0;\n    let frameCount = 0;\n\n    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\n      let hasErrored =
